raw_consumer_statement: On XX/XX/scrub> 2025, I received an email from XXXX in the XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX at Barclays claiming the merchant contacted me on XX/XX/XXXX and XX/XX/XXXX, but I have confirmed with my phone provider that no such calls occurred. Barclays has not provided any proof of these calls. I also submitted evidence via mail, including emails showing I reached out to the merchant on XX/XX/year>2025, to return the item. The merchant never responded. Despite this, Barclays still claims I didnt try to resolve the issue, which shows they did not properly review the documents I sent. The merchant does not allow return unless you cintact them and get a label from them. The merchant seems to be only be able to reach out to my bank but not me which I find to be suspicious. Furthermore, a letter dated XX/XX/scrub> 2025, was not received until XX/XX/XXXX, yet it stated I had 10 days from the letter date to respond. Another issue is that XXXX XX/XX/XXXX email had the wrong address listed, even though Barclays previously used the correct one. I believe Barclays is acting in bad faith by ignoring evidence, making false claims, and being inconsistent with communication. I am requesting a full review and resolution of this matter. Documents were previously mailed and are attached again here for reference.
